sections.section_heading: Scope

chunks.text: This policy governs Company support for remote and hybrid work arrangements, including one-time home office setup and ongoing monthly stipends. It does not govern travel and is not part of the Travel & Expense Policy framework.

sections.section_heading: Employees in roles formally designated as Remote or Hybrid a

chunks.text: Employees in roles formally designated as Remote or Hybrid are eligible for this stipend. Roles designated as In-Office are not eligible.

sections.section_heading: The role designation is recorded in the employee's HR record

chunks.text: The role designation is recorded in the employee's HR record and may be changed only with the agreement of the employee's manager and HR.

sections.section_heading: New remote and hybrid employees are eligible for a one-time

chunks.text: New remote and hybrid employees are eligible for a one-time home office setup allowance of $1,500 (USD), to be used within the first 90 days of employment in the eligible role.

sections.section_heading: The allowance may be used for:

chunks.text: • Desk • Office chair • Monitor (single, up to 32 inches) • Keyboard and mouse • Webcam and microphone • Desk lamp • Cable management and small accessories

sections.section_heading: The following are not eligible under the one-time allowance:

chunks.text: • Computers, tablets, and phones (provided separately by IT) • Office furniture beyond the desk-and-chair pairing (e.g., bookshelves, filing cabinets) • Decor, plants, or aesthetic items • Software and subscriptions

sections.section_heading: Receipts are required for all purchases under this allowance

chunks.text: Receipts are required for all purchases under this allowance, per the standard receipt requirements (TEP-007).

sections.section_heading: Eligible employees receive a monthly remote-work stipend of

chunks.text: Eligible employees receive a monthly remote-work stipend of $80, intended to offset internet, electricity, and basic supplies costs associated with working from home.

sections.section_heading: The monthly stipend is paid through payroll, is not a reimbu

chunks.text: The monthly stipend is paid through payroll, is not a reimbursement, and does not require receipts.

sections.section_heading: The stipend is treated as taxable income to the employee, co

chunks.text: The stipend is treated as taxable income to the employee, consistent with applicable tax rules.

sections.section_heading: Items purchased under the one-time setup allowance remain th

chunks.text: Items purchased under the one-time setup allowance remain the employee's property and do not need to be returned at end of employment, provided the employee has been in the eligible role for at least 12 months.

sections.section_heading: Employees who separate from the Company within 12 months of

chunks.text: Employees who separate from the Company within 12 months of receiving the setup allowance are subject to pro-rata recapture of the allowance through final-payroll deduction.

sections.section_heading: The Remote Work Stipend is distinct from travel reimbursemen

chunks.text: The Remote Work Stipend is distinct from travel reimbursements. Travel between the employee's home and a Company office is generally considered commuting (not reimbursable per TEP-006 §4.2), unless the employee is designated Remote and the office visit is at the Company's specific request.

sections.section_heading: When a Remote employee is asked by the Company to travel to

chunks.text: When a Remote employee is asked by the Company to travel to a Company office for a meeting, training, or event, the trip is treated as business travel and is reimbursed under the standard framework.

sections.section_heading: Definitions

chunks.text: • Remote — A role designation where the employee primarily works from a non-Company location and is not expected to be in an office regularly. • Hybrid — A role designation where the employee splits time between a Company office and a non-Company location, typically with a minimum in-office cadence set by the manager. • In-Office — A role designation where the employee is expected to be at a Company office during normal working hours. • Setup allowance — The one-time allowance for home office equipment described in §3.

sections.section_heading: Purpose

chunks.text: Northwind Logistics is committed to maintaining a workplace where every employee is treated with dignity and respect, has equal access to opportunity, and can contribute their best work without facing discrimination, harassment, or retaliation. This policy describes the standards of conduct expected of every person on Company premises and the procedures available to those who experience or witness violations.

sections.section_heading: Scope

chunks.text: This policy applies in every Company workplace — corporate offices, distribution centers, customer sites, Company vehicles, and Company-sponsored events. It applies to in-person and remote interactions, including communications via email, chat, video conference, and social media.

sections.section_heading: The Company prohibits discrimination against employees and a

chunks.text: The Company prohibits discrimination against employees and applicants on the basis of race, color, ethnicity, national origin, ancestry, religion, sex, gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, marital status, pregnancy, age, disability, genetic information, military or veteran status, or any other classification protected by applicable law.

sections.section_heading: Hiring, compensation, promotion, training, discipline, and t

chunks.text: Hiring, compensation, promotion, training, discipline, and termination decisions are made on the basis of legitimate, job-related factors.

sections.section_heading: The Company complies with all applicable laws regarding equa

chunks.text: The Company complies with all applicable laws regarding equal opportunity, including in the United States: Title VII of the Civil Rights Act, the Americans with Disabilities Act, the Age Discrimination in Employment Act, the Equal Pay Act, the Pregnancy Discrimination Act, and state and local equivalents.

sections.section_heading: Harassment is unwelcome conduct based on a protected charact

chunks.text: Harassment is unwelcome conduct based on a protected characteristic that creates a hostile, intimidating, or offensive work environment, or that affects employment decisions.

sections.section_heading: Examples include but are not limited to: slurs or jokes targ

chunks.text: Examples include but are not limited to: slurs or jokes targeting a protected characteristic, displaying offensive material, unwanted physical contact, sexual advances, propositions, or pressure for personal interactions.

sections.section_heading: Sexual harassment is a specific form of harassment and is pr

chunks.text: Sexual harassment is a specific form of harassment and is prohibited regardless of the genders or relationship of the parties involved.

sections.section_heading: Even where conduct does not rise to the level of unlawful ha

chunks.text: Even where conduct does not rise to the level of unlawful harassment, the Company expects civility. Bullying — repeated unreasonable conduct directed at an employee that intimidates, humiliates, or undermines — is prohibited.

sections.section_heading: Disagreement, criticism, and performance feedback delivered

chunks.text: Disagreement, criticism, and performance feedback delivered respectfully are not bullying.

sections.section_heading: The Company provides reasonable accommodation to employees a

chunks.text: The Company provides reasonable accommodation to employees and applicants with disabilities, sincerely held religious beliefs, pregnancy-related conditions, or other qualifying needs.

sections.section_heading: Accommodation requests should be directed to HR or through t

chunks.text: Accommodation requests should be directed to HR or through the Workday accommodation portal.

sections.section_heading: Employees who experience or witness conduct violating this p

chunks.text: Employees who experience or witness conduct violating this policy are encouraged to report it through any of: direct manager, skip-level manager, Human Resources, the General Counsel, or the anonymous Ethics Hotline (operated by an independent third party).

sections.section_heading: Reports may be made anonymously

chunks.text: Reports may be made anonymously. The Company treats reports confidentially to the extent practical given the need to investigate.

sections.section_heading: Reports are investigated promptly, typically within 30 days,

chunks.text: Reports are investigated promptly, typically within 30 days, by HR or a designated investigator independent of the alleged conduct.

sections.section_heading: Investigators may interview involved parties, review relevan

chunks.text: Investigators may interview involved parties, review relevant documents, and consult subject- matter experts.

sections.section_heading: The Company communicates investigation outcomes to the repor

chunks.text: The Company communicates investigation outcomes to the reporter and the subject of the report to the extent permitted by privacy considerations and applicable law.

sections.section_heading: The Company prohibits retaliation against any employee who,

chunks.text: The Company prohibits retaliation against any employee who, in good faith, reports a concern, participates in an investigation, or refuses to participate in conduct that violates this policy.

sections.section_heading: Retaliation includes adverse employment action, exclusion, a

chunks.text: Retaliation includes adverse employment action, exclusion, and informal mistreatment.

sections.section_heading: Retaliation is itself a violation of this policy and may res

chunks.text: Retaliation is itself a violation of this policy and may result in disciplinary action up to termination.

sections.section_heading: Discipline

chunks.text: Violations of this policy result in disciplinary action proportionate to the severity, including written warning, performance plan, demotion, or termination. The Company also reserves the right to pursue civil or criminal remedies where warranted.

sections.section_heading: All employees complete inclusion and anti-harassment trainin

chunks.text: All employees complete inclusion and anti-harassment training within 30 days of hire and annually thereafter.

sections.section_heading: People managers complete additional manager-specific trainin

chunks.text: People managers complete additional manager-specific training covering reporting obligations, accommodation processes, and difficult conversations.

sections.section_heading: Definitions

chunks.text: • Protected characteristic — A characteristic identified in Section 3.1 or under applicable law. • Adverse employment action — A material change in the terms or conditions of employment, including termination, demotion, denial of promotion, significant reduction in responsibilities, or material change in compensation. • Good faith — Acting on a reasonable belief in the truth of one's statements, regardless of whether the statements ultimately prove correct.

sections.section_heading: Purpose

chunks.text: The Employee Referral Program (ERP) recognizes that employees are often best positioned to identify strong candidates for open Company roles. This document describes the program structure, referral process, bonuses, and the conditions under which bonuses are paid.

sections.section_heading: All full-time and part-time employees of Northwind Logistics

chunks.text: All full-time and part-time employees of Northwind Logistics in active status are eligible to refer candidates, with the following exceptions: • Members of the Talent Acquisition team • Hiring managers for the specific role being filled • The candidate's potential direct manager • Members of the executive team (Grade 9+) — eligible to refer but not to receive a bonus

sections.section_heading: Contractors and temporary employees are not eligible to part

chunks.text: Contractors and temporary employees are not eligible to participate.

sections.section_heading: To qualify for a referral bonus, the candidate must:

chunks.text: • Not have applied to the same role independently in the prior 90 days • Not be currently employed by Northwind Logistics or its affiliates • Not have been employed by Northwind Logistics or its affiliates within the prior 12 months (rehires are managed separately) • Be referred via the ERP portal (referrals via personal email or LinkedIn DM without portal submission do not qualify)

sections.section_heading: Submit a referral via the ERP portal at careers.northwindlog

chunks.text: Submit a referral via the ERP portal at careers.northwindlogistics.com/referral, including the candidate's name, email, the role of interest, and (optionally) the referrer's notes about candidate fit.

sections.section_heading: The candidate receives an invitation email to apply through

chunks.text: The candidate receives an invitation email to apply through the standard application flow, with the referral attribution captured automatically.

sections.section_heading: Once the candidate applies, the standard recruiting process

chunks.text: Once the candidate applies, the standard recruiting process proceeds. The referrer may be asked for additional context but does not participate in interviews or decisions.

sections.section_heading: Bonus amounts by role

chunks.text: Role band Bonus (USD, gross) Individual contributor — Grades 1–4 $2,500 Individual contributor — Grades 5–6 $4,000 Manager / Senior IC — Grades 5–7 $5,000 Director — Grade 7+ $7,500 Vice President — Grade 9+ $12,500 Strategic / hard-to-fill roles (designated by recruiting) Up to $15,000

sections.section_heading: Bonuses are paid in two installments:

chunks.text: • 50% on the candidate's start date • 50% after the candidate has been employed for 90 calendar days in good standing

sections.section_heading: Bonuses are paid through payroll and are subject to applicab

chunks.text: Bonuses are paid through payroll and are subject to applicable taxes.

sections.section_heading: Multiple referrers

chunks.text: If multiple employees independently refer the same candidate prior to the candidate's application, the first attributed referral receives the bonus.

sections.section_heading: Bonus forfeiture

chunks.text: The bonus is forfeited if: • The referrer leaves the Company before either payment milestone • The candidate leaves the Company before the 90-day milestone (second installment only) • The referral is found to have been made in violation of this policy or other Company policies

sections.section_heading: The Company periodically designates certain underrepresented

chunks.text: The Company periodically designates certain underrepresented-talent referrals for enhanced bonuses, typically a 50% uplift above the standard amount.

sections.section_heading: Enhanced bonuses are not automatic; the recruiting team iden

chunks.text: Enhanced bonuses are not automatic; the recruiting team identifies eligible referrals based on self-disclosed demographic information consistent with applicable law.

sections.section_heading: Referrals of family members, household members, or close per

chunks.text: Referrals of family members, household members, or close personal relations must be disclosed in the referral submission. Whether to advance such referrals is at the recruiting team's discretion in consultation with HR.

sections.section_heading: Referrals creating a potential reporting relationship (e.g.,

chunks.text: Referrals creating a potential reporting relationship (e.g., the referrer would become the candidate's direct manager) generally are not eligible for bonus, even if the hire proceeds.

sections.section_heading: Tax treatment

chunks.text: Referral bonuses are taxable income to the referrer and are reported through payroll consistent with applicable tax rules.

sections.section_heading: Privacy

chunks.text: Candidate information submitted via the ERP is handled consistent with the Candidate Privacy Notice and is not disclosed to other employees beyond the recruiting and hiring team for the role.

sections.section_heading: Referrers may not represent themselves to candidates as havi

chunks.text: Referrers may not represent themselves to candidates as having authority over the hiring decision, salary, or other employment terms.

sections.section_heading: Pressuring candidates to apply, sharing confidential Company

chunks.text: Pressuring candidates to apply, sharing confidential Company information with candidates pre- employment, or making representations beyond publicly available role information is prohibited.

sections.section_heading: Use of the ERP to refer candidates for the purpose of genera

chunks.text: Use of the ERP to refer candidates for the purpose of generating referral bonuses without a good-faith belief in candidate fit is prohibited and may result in disciplinary action.

sections.section_heading: Definitions

chunks.text: • Referrer — An eligible employee who submits a candidate via the ERP portal. • Active status — Employed by the Company, not on a performance improvement plan that explicitly suspends eligibility for incentive programs. • Designated strategic role — A role flagged by Talent Acquisition leadership as eligible for an enhanced bonus due to difficulty of fill or business priority.

chunks.text: Records Retention Schedule Document: REC-001 Version: 2.0 Effective Date: January 1, 2025 Owner: General Counsel Applies To: All Northwind Logistics employees responsible for creating or maintaining business records

sections.section_heading: Purpose

chunks.text: This schedule defines how long the Company retains different categories of business records and the manner of disposition at the end of the retention period. Adherence supports compliance with applicable tax, audit, employment, and litigation-hold obligations, and reduces unnecessary storage of stale or sensitive information.

sections.section_heading: Scope

chunks.text: The schedule covers all business records of the Company, regardless of medium (paper, electronic, image, voice). It does not cover personal records of employees, transitory communications, or duplicates of officially retained records.

sections.section_heading: Financial records

chunks.text: Record Retention period Disposition Secure General ledger and supporting journals 10 years destruction Financial statements (audited) Permanent Archive Secure Tax returns and supporting documents 7 years from filing destruction Secure Accounts payable invoices and POs 7 years destruction Secure Accounts receivable invoices 7 years from collection or write-off destruction Employee expense reports and Secure

sections.section_heading: years from submission

chunks.text: supporting receipts destruction Secure Bank statements and reconciliations 7 years destruction Record Retention period Disposition

sections.section_heading: years (federal); longer where state Secure

chunks.text: Payroll registers law requires destruction

sections.section_heading: Employment records

chunks.text: Record Retention period Personnel files (active employee) Duration of employment Personnel files (former employee) 7 years post-termination I-9 employment authorization 3 years from hire or 1 year from termination, whichever is later Benefits enrollment 6 years Workers' compensation claims 30 years (or per state law) Performance reviews 3 years Training completion records 5 years

sections.section_heading: Legal and regulatory records

chunks.text: Record Retention period Executed contracts 7 years post-termination Litigation and investigation files Per litigation hold; otherwise 7 years post-resolution Regulatory filings Permanent Insurance policies 10 years post-policy-period Real-estate leases 10 years post-termination

sections.section_heading: Operational records

chunks.text: Record Retention period Shipping records and freight invoices 7 years Customer orders and order acknowledgments 7 years Vendor records (active vendor) Duration of relationship Vendor records (former vendor) 7 years post-offboarding Quality / compliance audit reports 7 years

sections.section_heading: Information technology records

chunks.text: Record Retention period System access logs 1 year Security incident logs 3 years (longer if litigation-related) Source code Duration of asset lifecycle + 3 years Backups Per Backup Policy (IT-405)

sections.section_heading: Secure destruction means destruction in a manner that render

chunks.text: Secure destruction means destruction in a manner that renders the record unreadable and unrecoverable. For paper, this means cross-cut shredding or pulping; for electronic media, secure wipe or physical destruction; for cloud-resident data, deletion through the platform's documented data-deletion process plus verification.

sections.section_heading: Archive means transfer to long-term off-site or cloud archiv

chunks.text: Archive means transfer to long-term off-site or cloud archival storage with limited access and documented retention beyond the active-record period.

sections.section_heading: Records under a litigation hold are exempt from this schedul

chunks.text: Records under a litigation hold are exempt from this schedule until the hold is lifted in writing by Legal. The litigation-hold obligation overrides any retention period in this schedule.

sections.section_heading: Department leaders are responsible for ensuring records unde

chunks.text: Department leaders are responsible for ensuring records under their stewardship are maintained per this schedule.

sections.section_heading: Annual record review and disposition events should be conduc

chunks.text: Annual record review and disposition events should be conducted in Q1 of each year, covering records that exceeded their retention period in the prior year.

sections.section_heading: Records Management staff are available to advise on classifi

chunks.text: Records Management staff are available to advise on classification and disposition questions and may be reached at records@northwindlogistics.com.

sections.section_heading: When litigation, investigation, or regulatory inquiry is rea

chunks.text: When litigation, investigation, or regulatory inquiry is reasonably anticipated, Legal will issue a written litigation hold notice to affected custodians. Records covered by the hold must be preserved regardless of this schedule.

sections.section_heading: Custodians must acknowledge receipt of litigation hold notic

chunks.text: Custodians must acknowledge receipt of litigation hold notices within 5 business days and confirm preservation measures.

sections.section_heading: Definitions

chunks.text: • Record — Information created, received, or maintained as evidence of a transaction, decision, or activity, in fulfillment of legal obligation or business need. • Transitory communications — Drafts, working notes, and routine emails of no enduring business value. • Litigation hold — A formal directive from Legal suspending normal retention disposal for specified records.

sections.section_heading: Purpose

chunks.text: The Company is committed to maintaining the highest standards of ethics, integrity, and legal compliance. This policy establishes the channels through which concerns about suspected violations may be raised and the protections available to those who raise them in good faith.

sections.section_heading: Scope

chunks.text: This policy covers concerns about suspected violations of law, regulation, the Company's Code of Conduct, or other Company policies, including but not limited to: financial misconduct, fraud, bribery and corruption, anti-trust violations, sanctions violations, environmental violations, harassment and discrimination, retaliation, and conflicts of interest.

sections.section_heading: Reporting channels

chunks.text: Concerns may be raised through any of the following channels:

sections.section_heading: Direct manager

chunks.text: Direct manager. Most concerns are best resolved through direct discussion with one's manager.

sections.section_heading: Skip-level manager

chunks.text: Skip-level manager. Where direct discussion is not appropriate or has not produced a satisfactory response, the manager's manager.

sections.section_heading: Human Resources

chunks.text: Human Resources. For concerns involving people-related matters, including discrimination, harassment, or workplace conduct.

sections.section_heading: General Counsel

chunks.text: General Counsel. For concerns involving legal or regulatory matters, contract violations, or fiduciary issues.

sections.section_heading: Internal Audit

chunks.text: Internal Audit. For concerns involving financial reporting, internal controls, or accounting matters.

sections.section_heading: Anonymous Ethics Hotline

chunks.text: Anonymous Ethics Hotline. A 24/7 hotline operated by an independent third-party vendor, accessible by phone (1-800-NW-ETHIC) or by web (ethics.northwindlogistics.com). Anonymous reporting is supported.

sections.section_heading: Reporters may elect to provide their name or to remain anony

chunks.text: Reporters may elect to provide their name or to remain anonymous. The Company encourages identified reporting because it permits more thorough investigation and follow-up, but anonymity is fully supported.

sections.section_heading: The identity of reporters who provide their name is treated

chunks.text: The identity of reporters who provide their name is treated as confidential to the extent practical given the need to investigate. Identity may be disclosed where required by law, by court order, or where it becomes unavoidable in the course of investigation.

sections.section_heading: Reports made in good faith are protected by this policy rega

chunks.text: Reports made in good faith are protected by this policy regardless of whether the underlying concern is ultimately substantiated.

sections.section_heading: Knowingly false reports made in bad faith are themselves a v

chunks.text: Knowingly false reports made in bad faith are themselves a violation of this policy and may result in disciplinary action.

sections.section_heading: The Company strictly prohibits retaliation against any perso

chunks.text: The Company strictly prohibits retaliation against any person who, in good faith, makes a report under this policy, participates in an investigation, or refuses to participate in conduct that they reasonably believe violates law, regulation, or Company policy.

sections.section_heading: Retaliation includes — but is not limited to — termination,

chunks.text: Retaliation includes — but is not limited to — termination, demotion, denial of promotion, material change in working conditions, exclusion from meetings or projects, and informal mistreatment.

sections.section_heading: Retaliation may itself be reported under this policy

chunks.text: Retaliation may itself be reported under this policy. Reports of retaliation are investigated with the same seriousness as the underlying concern.

sections.section_heading: Reports are reviewed by the Ethics Office (within the Office

chunks.text: Reports are reviewed by the Ethics Office (within the Office of the General Counsel) within 5 business days of receipt and assigned to an appropriate investigator.

sections.section_heading: Investigations are conducted by individuals independent of t

chunks.text: Investigations are conducted by individuals independent of the alleged conduct and trained in investigation methodology.

sections.section_heading: Investigations may involve interviewing the reporter (where

chunks.text: Investigations may involve interviewing the reporter (where identified), the subject of the report, witnesses, and other relevant parties; reviewing documents and records; and consulting subject- matter experts.

sections.section_heading: Investigations are completed as promptly as the facts permit

chunks.text: Investigations are completed as promptly as the facts permit. Typical timelines are 30–60 days; complex matters may take longer.

sections.section_heading: To the extent permitted by privacy considerations and applic

chunks.text: To the extent permitted by privacy considerations and applicable law, the reporter (where identified) and the subject are informed of the investigation outcome.

sections.section_heading: In the United States, multiple statutes provide additional l

chunks.text: In the United States, multiple statutes provide additional legal protections for whistleblowers, including the Sarbanes-Oxley Act, the Dodd-Frank Act, the False Claims Act, and various sector- specific laws.

sections.section_heading: Nothing in this policy or any Company agreement is intended

chunks.text: Nothing in this policy or any Company agreement is intended to limit a reporter's right to communicate concerns to government regulators, including the SEC, OSHA, the Department of Labor, the DOJ, or equivalent state and international authorities. Such communications may be made without prior Company notice or consent.

sections.section_heading: Awards or bounties available under whistleblower laws (e.g.,

chunks.text: Awards or bounties available under whistleblower laws (e.g., SEC whistleblower program) are not affected by this policy.

sections.section_heading: The Audit Committee of the Board of Directors receives a qua

chunks.text: The Audit Committee of the Board of Directors receives a quarterly summary of reports and investigations, with detail proportionate to the matter's significance.

sections.section_heading: The Company contracts with a third-party ethics hotline prov

chunks.text: The Company contracts with a third-party ethics hotline provider; the contract requires the provider to maintain the confidentiality and anonymity options of the channel.

sections.section_heading: Definitions

chunks.text: • Good faith — Acting on a reasonable belief in the truth or significance of one's statements, regardless of whether they prove correct. • Retaliation — Adverse action taken against a person because of their good-faith engagement with this policy. • Ethics Office — A function within the Office of the General Counsel responsible for receiving and triaging reports under this policy.

sections.section_heading: Purpose

chunks.text: This notice describes how the Company collects, uses, shares, and protects personal information about customers, prospects, and other natural persons in connection with its business operations. It supports the Company's compliance with applicable privacy laws and reflects the privacy expectations of the people whose information we handle.

sections.section_heading: Scope

chunks.text: This notice applies to processing of personal information by the Company and its affiliates, in connection with: provision of logistics services, customer support, marketing, payment processing, customer communications, and operational analytics. This notice does not apply to: processing of employee personal information (governed by the Employee Privacy Notice), processing on behalf of customers as a data processor (governed by the customer's privacy notice and any data processing addendum), and de-identified or aggregate data not capable of identifying an individual.

sections.section_heading: Categories of personal information

chunks.text: Category Examples Source Name, customer ID, email, phone, Provided by customer; observed in Identifiers business address interactions Commercial Shipment history, purchase records, Generated through service information contract terms provision Provided by customer; via Financial information Payment method, billing records payment processor IP address, device type, browser, Observed when using our digital Technical information cookies properties Category Examples Source Communication Emails, chat transcripts, call Generated through customer content recordings (where lawful) interactions CCTV at facilities receiving customer Sensory data Observed pickups

sections.section_heading: Collection methods

chunks.text: We collect personal information when customers contact us, request services, place orders, register accounts, attend events, or visit our facilities or digital properties.

sections.section_heading: Children's data

chunks.text: We do not knowingly collect personal information from children under 16. If we learn that we have done so inadvertently, we delete it promptly.

sections.section_heading: How we use information

chunks.text: We use personal information to: • Provide and improve logistics services • Communicate with customers about their orders, accounts, and service issues • Process payments and resolve disputes • Comply with legal obligations (tax, regulatory, customs) • Detect and prevent fraud, security incidents, and policy violations • Conduct analytics to understand and improve customer experience • Engage in marketing communications, with appropriate consent where required

sections.section_heading: Sharing of information

chunks.text: We share personal information only as needed and with appropriate safeguards. Categories of recipients include: • Service providers (cloud hosting, payment processors, fraud prevention, customer support tooling) under data processing agreements • Logistics partners and carriers, where the customer's order requires it • Affiliates, for purposes consistent with this notice • Authorities, in response to lawful requests or to comply with legal obligations • Acquirers, in connection with a business transaction (with continued notice obligation) We do not sell personal information for monetary consideration.
We do not share personal information for third-party advertising in a manner that would constitute "sale" or "sharing" under California or other state law without offering an opt-out.

sections.section_heading: International transfers

chunks.text: The Company operates globally.

Personal information may be transferred to and processed in countries other than the country of collection. Where required, the Company uses Standard Contractual Clauses, adequacy decisions, or other lawful transfer mechanisms.

sections.section_heading: Security

chunks.text: The Company maintains administrative, technical, and physical safeguards designed to protect personal information against unauthorized access, alteration, disclosure, or destruction. These include access controls, encryption, audit logging, employee training, and incident response procedures. See the Data Classification standard (SEC-201) for related controls.

sections.section_heading: Retention

chunks.text: We retain personal information for as long as necessary for the purposes described in this notice, consistent with the Records Retention Schedule (REC-001) and applicable legal requirements.

sections.section_heading: Individual rights

chunks.text: Depending on the jurisdiction of residence, individuals may have rights to: • Access the personal information we hold about them • Correct inaccurate information • Request deletion (subject to legal exceptions) • Object to or restrict certain processing • Receive a portable copy of their information • Opt out of marketing communications • Withdraw consent Requests may be submitted to privacy@northwindlogistics.com or via the Privacy Request portal at privacy.northwindlogistics.com. We respond within the timelines required by applicable law (typically 30–45 days; extendable with notice in complex cases).

sections.section_heading: Verification of requests

chunks.text: To protect personal information from unauthorized requests, we verify the identity of requestors. Verification methods are proportionate to the sensitivity of the information requested.

sections.section_heading: Authorized agents

chunks.text: Individuals in California and certain other jurisdictions may designate an authorized agent to make requests on their behalf, subject to verification of the agent's authority.

sections.section_heading: Cookies and tracking

chunks.text: The Company's digital properties use cookies and similar technologies. The Cookie Notice on our website describes the categories of cookies used, the purposes, the retention period, and how to manage preferences.

sections.section_heading: Changes to this notice

chunks.text: We may update this notice as our practices or legal obligations change. Material changes are communicated by posting the updated notice and, where required, by direct notice to affected individuals.

sections.section_heading: Contact

chunks.text: Privacy questions, concerns, and rights requests: Email: privacy@northwindlogistics.com Mail: Privacy Office, Northwind Logistics, 200 Spectrum Center Dr, Irvine CA 92618 USA EU representative: As required, designated representative under GDPR Art. 27 is identified at privacy.northwindlogistics.com/eu-representative

sections.section_heading: Purpose

chunks.text: This policy describes the Company's ownership of intellectual property developed in the course of employment, the protection of proprietary information, the use of third-party intellectual property, and the obligations of employees in connection with the Company's IP and that of others.

sections.section_heading: Scope

chunks.text: The policy applies to all forms of intellectual property — copyrights, patents, trademarks, trade secrets, and confidential know-how — and to all employees, contractors, and authorized agents of the Company globally.

sections.section_heading: Work made for hire

chunks.text: Work made for hire. Subject to applicable law, all intellectual property created by an employee in the course of employment, using Company resources, or related to the Company's business, is the property of the Company.

sections.section_heading: Assignment

chunks.text: Assignment. Where the doctrine of "work made for hire" does not assign IP automatically, employees assign such IP to the Company by their employment agreement and acknowledgement of this policy.

sections.section_heading: Exclusions

chunks.text: Exclusions.

IP developed by an employee entirely on their own time, without Company resources, and unrelated to the Company's business or anticipated business, is generally the employee's. Employees in California, Washington, and certain other states have specific statutory protections; the employment agreement reflects these.

sections.section_heading: Inventions made during employment are disclosed to the Compa

chunks.text: Inventions made during employment are disclosed to the Company via the Invention Disclosure form in the Self-Service Portal.

sections.section_heading: The Company's Patent Committee reviews invention disclosures

chunks.text: The Company's Patent Committee reviews invention disclosures and decides on patent prosecution.

sections.section_heading: Inventors named on issued Company patents receive a patent i

chunks.text: Inventors named on issued Company patents receive a patent issuance award per the Inventor Recognition Program.

sections.section_heading: The Company permits and encourages thoughtful use of open-so

chunks.text: The Company permits and encourages thoughtful use of open-source software in Company products and operations, subject to the Open Source Software Standard (LEG-204).

sections.section_heading: Use of open-source software in a Company product requires li

chunks.text: Use of open-source software in a Company product requires license review by Legal prior to inclusion.

sections.section_heading: Strong copyleft licenses (e.g., GPLv3, AGPL) require additio

chunks.text: Strong copyleft licenses (e.g., GPLv3, AGPL) require additional review and are generally restricted to internal-only use.

sections.section_heading: Employee contributions to open-source projects require pre-a

chunks.text: Employee contributions to open-source projects require pre-approval from the employee's manager and Legal, particularly where the contribution involves code developed during employment or relating to the Company's business.

sections.section_heading: Use of Company names, logos, and trademarks must follow the

chunks.text: Use of Company names, logos, and trademarks must follow the Brand Guidelines (BRAND-001).

sections.section_heading: External communications using Company branding (presentation

chunks.text: External communications using Company branding (presentations, customer-facing documents, conference materials) should be reviewed for brand compliance.

sections.section_heading: Adoption of new product names or product trademarks requires

chunks.text: Adoption of new product names or product trademarks requires Legal clearance to avoid infringing third-party rights.

sections.section_heading: The Company's trade secrets include — but are not limited to

chunks.text: The Company's trade secrets include — but are not limited to — pricing strategy, customer lists, vendor terms, source code, proprietary algorithms, operational know-how, and unannounced product or facility plans.

sections.section_heading: Employees must not disclose, use, or take Company trade secr

chunks.text: Employees must not disclose, use, or take Company trade secrets outside the scope of their work, whether during employment or after.

sections.section_heading: Proprietary information remains the Company's property at th

chunks.text: Proprietary information remains the Company's property at the end of employment. Departing employees must return all materials and confirm that they have not retained Company information (including by employee certification).

sections.section_heading: Use of third-party intellectual property in Company products

chunks.text: Use of third-party intellectual property in Company products or operations requires a valid license, public-domain status, or fair-use justification reviewed by Legal.

sections.section_heading: Stock images, fonts, music, and similar assets used in Compa

chunks.text: Stock images, fonts, music, and similar assets used in Company materials must come from properly licensed sources. Internal stock libraries are available via the brand portal.

sections.section_heading: Use of customer information, vendor information, or other th

chunks.text: Use of customer information, vendor information, or other third-party information held in confidence is constrained by the relevant agreement; misuse may breach both contract and this policy.

sections.section_heading: Outputs of generative AI tools may incorporate or be derived

chunks.text: Outputs of generative AI tools may incorporate or be derived from third-party IP. Care must be taken to verify the licensing implications of using AI-generated content in Company products.

sections.section_heading: Inputs to public AI tools should not include Company Confide

chunks.text: Inputs to public AI tools should not include Company Confidential or Restricted data (see SEC-201 and SEC-202).

sections.section_heading: The Generative AI Use Standard (LEG-205) provides detailed g

chunks.text: The Generative AI Use Standard (LEG-205) provides detailed guidance.

sections.section_heading: Suspected unauthorized use of Company IP by third parties sh

chunks.text: Suspected unauthorized use of Company IP by third parties should be reported to ip@northwindlogistics.com.

sections.section_heading: Notices alleging that the Company is infringing third-party

chunks.text: Notices alleging that the Company is infringing third-party IP should be forwarded immediately to Legal without response by the receiving employee.

sections.section_heading: Departing employees are reminded of their continuing confide

chunks.text: Departing employees are reminded of their continuing confidentiality and assignment obligations.

sections.section_heading: Employees going to work for a competitor or to start a compe

chunks.text: Employees going to work for a competitor or to start a competing business have specific restrictions in their employment agreement (where enforceable under applicable law) and continuing obligations regardless.

sections.section_heading: Definitions

chunks.text: • Work made for hire — Under U.S. copyright law, a work prepared by an employee within the scope of employment, with the employer as legal author. • Trade secret — Information that derives independent economic value from not being generally known and is the subject of reasonable measures to maintain secrecy. • Copyleft — A licensing model requiring derivative works to be made available under the same license, restricting proprietary use.

chunks.text: Data Classification and Handling Standard Document: SEC-201 Version: 2.3 Effective Date: January 1, 2025 Owner: Information Security Applies To: All Northwind Logistics employees, contractors, and systems

sections.section_heading: Purpose

chunks.text: This standard defines how the Company classifies data assets by sensitivity and the controls required for each classification. Adherence is mandatory and is a precondition for handling Company data.

sections.section_heading: Scope

chunks.text: The standard covers all data created, received, processed, transmitted, or stored on behalf of the Company, in any medium — electronic, paper, image, voice, or other — across any platform, device, or environment.

sections.section_heading: Public

chunks.text: Data approved for public release.
Disclosure carries no risk to the Company. Examples: published press releases, marketing collateral, regulatory filings already public, the Company website.

sections.section_heading: Internal

chunks.text: Data intended for internal use whose unauthorized disclosure would cause limited harm. Examples: internal organizational charts, project plans, the employee directory, non-financial internal communications, internal training materials.

sections.section_heading: Confidential

chunks.text: Data whose unauthorized disclosure could cause material harm to the Company, its customers, or its employees. Examples: customer lists, contracts, pricing strategy, unannounced product or facility decisions, personnel files, employee compensation, internal financial reports, audit findings.

sections.section_heading: Restricted

chunks.text: Data whose unauthorized disclosure could cause severe harm or significant regulatory liability. Examples: payment card data (PAN), Social Security numbers, government-issued ID numbers, source code for proprietary systems, M&A transaction data, customer data subject to specific contractual protections.

sections.section_heading: Access

chunks.text: Classification Access principle Public No restriction Internal All employees by default; contractors with NDA Confidential Need-to-know basis; access reviewed quarterly Restricted Named individuals only; access reviewed monthly; multi-party approval to grant

sections.section_heading: Storage

chunks.text: Classification Storage requirement Public Any Internal Approved corporate platforms Confidential Approved corporate platforms with audit logging Restricted Approved corporate platforms with audit logging and encryption at rest

sections.section_heading: Transmission

chunks.text: Classification Transmission requirement Public Any Internal Encrypted in transit (TLS or equivalent) Confidential Encrypted in transit; no transmission to personal email or unmanaged endpoints Restricted Encrypted in transit and end-to-end; transmission requires DLP exception ticket

sections.section_heading: Retention

chunks.text: Retention requirements are governed by the Records Retention Schedule (REC-001). Where the retention schedule and this standard conflict, the schedule controls.

sections.section_heading: Labeling

chunks.text: Labeling. Documents at Confidential or Restricted classification should be labeled at the top of each page and in the document properties.

sections.section_heading: Sharing externally

chunks.text: Sharing externally.
External sharing of Confidential data requires a signed NDA. External sharing of Restricted data requires Legal and Security approval.

sections.section_heading: Printing

chunks.text: Printing. Restricted data should not be printed except where required by law or regulator request, in which case the printed copy must be retrieved immediately from the printer and securely destroyed when no longer needed.

sections.section_heading: Mobile devices

chunks.text: Mobile devices. Confidential and Restricted data on mobile devices requires Mobile Device Management (MDM) enrollment.

sections.section_heading: AI and analytics tools

chunks.text: AI and analytics tools. Confidential and Restricted data may not be submitted to public LLM tools (ChatGPT, public Claude, etc.) or external analytics platforms not on the Approved Tools list.

sections.section_heading: Incident reporting

chunks.text: Suspected loss, theft, exposure, or unauthorized access of Confidential or Restricted data must be reported to the Security team within 4 hours of discovery, via security@northwindlogistics.com or the on-call security number.

sections.section_heading: Exceptions

chunks.text: Exceptions to specific controls in this standard require approval by the Chief Information Security Officer, documented in writing, and reviewed at least annually.

sections.section_heading: Definitions

chunks.text: • Data subject — A natural person to whom personal data relates. • Audit logging — System logging that records who accessed what data, when, and from where. • Encryption at rest — Encryption of stored data such that ciphertext is unintelligible without keys held separately from the storage. • DLP — Data Loss Prevention, a class of controls that monitors and blocks unauthorized data movement.

sections.section_heading: Purpose

chunks.text: This policy establishes acceptable use of the Company's information technology resources and the conduct expected of users. Adherence is a condition of access.

sections.section_heading: Scope

chunks.text: The policy applies to all Company-owned, leased, or operated information technology resources, including: computers, mobile devices, accounts, networks (wired, wireless, VPN), email and messaging systems, file storage, cloud services, and any service accessed using Company credentials. It applies whether the user is on Company premises, at home, traveling, or elsewhere.

sections.section_heading: Business purpose

chunks.text: Business purpose. Company IT resources are provided for business purposes.
Limited and occasional personal use that does not interfere with work, consume material resources, or violate other policies is acceptable.

sections.section_heading: No expectation of privacy

chunks.text: No expectation of privacy. The Company reserves the right to monitor, review, and audit use of its IT resources, including content of files, communications, and browsing history, as permitted by applicable law.

sections.section_heading: No transfer

chunks.text: No transfer.
User accounts may not be shared. Users are responsible for activity occurring under their accounts.

sections.section_heading: All Company accounts must be protected by passwords meeting

chunks.text: All Company accounts must be protected by passwords meeting current strength requirements (minimum 14 characters; complexity per the Password Standard SEC-203).

sections.section_heading: Multi-factor authentication is required for all Company acco

chunks.text: Multi-factor authentication is required for all Company accounts where supported. Disabling MFA is prohibited.

sections.section_heading: Credentials must not be stored in plaintext, written down in

chunks.text: Credentials must not be stored in plaintext, written down in unsecured locations, or shared via unsecured channels.

sections.section_heading: Company-issued devices must enroll in Mobile Device Manageme

chunks.text: Company-issued devices must enroll in Mobile Device Management (MDM) and remain enrolled throughout their use.

sections.section_heading: Personal devices used to access Company resources must enrol

chunks.text: Personal devices used to access Company resources must enroll in MDM for the Company partition and meet baseline security configuration (OS patches current, full-disk encryption enabled, screen lock enabled).

sections.section_heading: Devices must be locked when unattended and powered off or lo

chunks.text: Devices must be locked when unattended and powered off or locked when traveling internationally.

sections.section_heading: The Company-managed Wi-Fi network is for Company devices and

chunks.text: The Company-managed Wi-Fi network is for Company devices and registered personal devices. Guest Wi-Fi is for visitors and personal use.

sections.section_heading: VPN must be used when accessing internal Company systems fro

chunks.text: VPN must be used when accessing internal Company systems from outside the corporate network, except for systems explicitly designated as accessible from the public internet.

sections.section_heading: Connection to untrusted networks (public Wi-Fi, hotel Wi-Fi,

chunks.text: Connection to untrusted networks (public Wi-Fi, hotel Wi-Fi, airport Wi-Fi) is permitted only via VPN.

sections.section_heading: Company email is for business communications

chunks.text: Company email is for business communications. Personal email accounts may not be used for Company business.

sections.section_heading: Phishing and impersonation attempts should be reported via t

chunks.text: Phishing and impersonation attempts should be reported via the "Report Phish" button in the email client or to security@northwindlogistics.com.

sections.section_heading: Sensitive information (Confidential or Restricted per SEC-20

chunks.text: Sensitive information (Confidential or Restricted per SEC-201) should not be sent in unencrypted email outside the Company.

sections.section_heading: Only approved software may be installed on Company devices

chunks.text: Only approved software may be installed on Company devices. The Approved Software list is maintained in the Self-Service Portal.

sections.section_heading: Installation of software requires either pre-approval (Appro

chunks.text: Installation of software requires either pre-approval (Approved Software list) or a Software Request Ticket.

sections.section_heading: Open-source components used in Company products are subject

chunks.text: Open-source components used in Company products are subject to the Open Source Policy (LEG-203).

sections.section_heading: Prohibited activities

chunks.text: The following are prohibited:

sections.section_heading: Use of Company IT resources to violate any law or other Comp

chunks.text: Use of Company IT resources to violate any law or other Company policy.

sections.section_heading: Installation or use of cryptocurrency mining software on Com

chunks.text: Installation or use of cryptocurrency mining software on Company resources.

sections.section_heading: Use of Company resources for personal commercial activity un

chunks.text: Use of Company resources for personal commercial activity unrelated to the Company.

sections.section_heading: Connecting unauthorized devices to Company networks (e.g., p

chunks.text: Connecting unauthorized devices to Company networks (e.g., personal routers, IoT devices).

sections.section_heading: Sharing accounts or credentials, including with other Compan

chunks.text: Sharing accounts or credentials, including with other Company employees.

sections.section_heading: Suspected security incidents must be reported to the Securit

chunks.text: Suspected security incidents must be reported to the Security team within 4 hours of discovery.

sections.section_heading: Lost or stolen devices must be reported immediately so that

chunks.text: Lost or stolen devices must be reported immediately so that remote wipe can be triggered.

sections.section_heading: Consequences

chunks.text: Violation of this policy may result in revocation of access, disciplinary action up to and including termination, and civil or criminal liability where applicable.

sections.section_heading: Definitions

chunks.text: • Approved Software — Software listed in the Self-Service Portal as preinstalled or available for self-install without ticket. • MDM — Mobile Device Management; a system enabling the Company to enforce configuration policy on enrolled devices. • VPN — Virtual Private Network; an encrypted connection between a remote device and the corporate network. • MFA — Multi-Factor Authentication.

sections.section_heading: Purpose

chunks.text: This document describes the safety practices, emergency procedures, and reporting expectations for all Company office facilities. The goal is a workplace that is safe by design and prepared for the range of foreseeable emergencies.

sections.section_heading: Scope

chunks.text: The procedures apply at all Company office locations. Distribution centers and operations facilities are covered by separate site-specific procedures supplementing this document.

sections.section_heading: All employees are issued an access badge upon hire

chunks.text: All employees are issued an access badge upon hire. Badges must be visible while on premises and surrendered at termination.

sections.section_heading: Tailgating (allowing another person to enter behind you with

chunks.text: Tailgating (allowing another person to enter behind you without their own badge) is prohibited.

sections.section_heading: Visitors must be sponsored by an employee, sign in at recept

chunks.text: Visitors must be sponsored by an employee, sign in at reception, and wear a visitor badge.

sections.section_heading: Each floor has clearly posted evacuation routes and assembly

chunks.text: Each floor has clearly posted evacuation routes and assembly points. Familiarize yourself with the nearest two exits from your workstation.

sections.section_heading: Fire alarms require immediate evacuation

chunks.text: Fire alarms require immediate evacuation. Do not return to the building until cleared by Facilities or the fire department.

sections.section_heading: Fire drills are conducted twice annually at each location

chunks.text: Fire drills are conducted twice annually at each location. Participation is mandatory.

sections.section_heading: Fire extinguishers are located at posted intervals

chunks.text: Fire extinguishers are located at posted intervals. Use only if trained and only when evacuation is in progress and the fire is small.

sections.section_heading: Call 911 (or local equivalent) immediately for serious medic

chunks.text: Call 911 (or local equivalent) immediately for serious medical emergencies.

sections.section_heading: Notify Reception so they can direct emergency responders to

chunks.text: Notify Reception so they can direct emergency responders to the location.

sections.section_heading: AEDs (automated external defibrillators) are located at each

chunks.text: AEDs (automated external defibrillators) are located at each floor's reception area and labeled. CPR-trained employees are listed on the floor's emergency contact poster.

sections.section_heading: First-aid kits are located at each floor's kitchen and recep

chunks.text: First-aid kits are located at each floor's kitchen and reception.

sections.section_heading: Tornado / severe wind warnings (where applicable): proceed t

chunks.text: Tornado / severe wind warnings (where applicable): proceed to designated shelter areas — interior corridors and stairwells away from exterior windows.

sections.section_heading: Earthquake (where applicable): Drop, Cover, Hold On

chunks.text: Earthquake (where applicable): Drop, Cover, Hold On. Stay where you are during shaking; evacuate only when shaking stops.

sections.section_heading: Severe winter weather affecting commute is announced via Wor

chunks.text: Severe winter weather affecting commute is announced via Workplace messaging by 6 AM local time. Office may be designated remote-only for the day.

sections.section_heading: All workplace injuries — no matter how minor — must be repor

chunks.text: All workplace injuries — no matter how minor — must be reported to EHS within 24 hours via the Workplace Incident form in the Self-Service Portal.

sections.section_heading: Work-related injuries are covered by the Company's workers'

chunks.text: Work-related injuries are covered by the Company's workers' compensation insurance. The form intake triggers the claim process.

sections.section_heading: Manager assistance is available; HR and EHS coordinate workp

chunks.text: Manager assistance is available; HR and EHS coordinate workplace accommodation and return- to-work planning.

sections.section_heading: Sit-stand desks are available at each workstation

chunks.text: Sit-stand desks are available at each workstation. Chairs are adjustable.

sections.section_heading: Employees experiencing repetitive strain or other ergonomic

chunks.text: Employees experiencing repetitive strain or other ergonomic discomfort may request an ergonomic assessment via EHS.

sections.section_heading: Approved ergonomic accessories (lumbar support, keyboard tra

chunks.text: Approved ergonomic accessories (lumbar support, keyboard tray, vertical mouse) are available through the Self-Service Portal.

sections.section_heading: Observed hazards — spills, broken equipment, blocked exits,

chunks.text: Observed hazards — spills, broken equipment, blocked exits, suspicious activity — should be reported to Facilities at facilities@northwindlogistics.com or via the Facilities Hotline.

sections.section_heading: Imminent hazards should be addressed by securing the area (c

chunks.text: Imminent hazards should be addressed by securing the area (cordoning off, posting a warning sign) and reporting immediately.

sections.section_heading: Hazardous materials

chunks.text: The Company does not maintain quantities of hazardous chemicals in office facilities beyond standard cleaning supplies. Where applicable, Safety Data Sheets are maintained at Reception and accessible to any employee on request.

sections.section_heading: The Company subscribes to "Run, Hide, Fight" guidance for ac

chunks.text: The Company subscribes to "Run, Hide, Fight" guidance for active threat situations. Familiarize yourself with the training module in the Learning portal.

sections.section_heading: Active threat alerts will be communicated via the emergency

chunks.text: Active threat alerts will be communicated via the emergency notification system (text + Workplace).

sections.section_heading: Do not attempt to coordinate or investigate

chunks.text: Do not attempt to coordinate or investigate. Follow instructions from responders.

sections.section_heading: Definitions

chunks.text: • Assembly point — A designated location outside the building where employees gather after evacuation for headcount. • Tailgating — Following another person through an access-controlled entry without using one's own badge. • AED — Automated External Defibrillator.

sections.section_heading: Purpose

chunks.text: This standard establishes the configuration, use, and security expectations for mobile devices — Company-issued and personally-owned — used to access Company resources. It supplements the general Acceptable Use Policy (SEC-202) with mobile-specific requirements.

sections.section_heading: Scope

chunks.text: The standard applies to smartphones, tablets, and other portable computing devices used to access Company email, files, applications, networks, or data, regardless of whether the device is owned by the Company or by the employee.

sections.section_heading: Devices purchased or leased by the Company and assigned to a

chunks.text: Devices purchased or leased by the Company and assigned to a specific employee.

sections.section_heading: Fully managed via Mobile Device Management (MDM)

chunks.text: Fully managed via Mobile Device Management (MDM). The Company has full configuration authority over these devices.

sections.section_heading: Permitted models: latest two generations of iPhone (Apple);

chunks.text: Permitted models: latest two generations of iPhone (Apple); latest two generations of Samsung Galaxy and Google Pixel (Android). Other models require an exception.

sections.section_heading: Personal devices used to access Company resources must enrol

chunks.text: Personal devices used to access Company resources must enroll the Company partition in MDM.

sections.section_heading: The Company's MDM enforces configuration on the Company part

chunks.text: The Company's MDM enforces configuration on the Company partition only. Personal apps and data on the device remain under the employee's control.

sections.section_heading: Personal devices used for BYOD must meet baseline requiremen

chunks.text: Personal devices used for BYOD must meet baseline requirements: - Current vendor-supported OS version - Manufacturer security patches current within 60 days - Screen lock with PIN, password, or biometric - Full-device encryption enabled - Compatible with the Company's MDM platform

sections.section_heading: Jailbroken or rooted devices may not access Company resource

chunks.text: Jailbroken or rooted devices may not access Company resources.

sections.section_heading: Screen lock with at least 6-digit PIN, alphanumeric password

chunks.text: Screen lock with at least 6-digit PIN, alphanumeric password, or biometric authentication; auto- lock after 5 minutes of inactivity.

sections.section_heading: Backup of Company data to a personal cloud (iCloud Backup, G

chunks.text: Backup of Company data to a personal cloud (iCloud Backup, Google Drive Backup, etc.) is disabled or scoped to exclude Company apps.

sections.section_heading: Find My Device or equivalent enabled, to support remote loca

chunks.text: Find My Device or equivalent enabled, to support remote location and wipe in the event of loss.

sections.section_heading: Approved corporate apps (email, calendar, document collabora

chunks.text: Approved corporate apps (email, calendar, document collaboration, chat, VPN) are installed via the corporate app catalog.

sections.section_heading: Other apps installed on the device must not access Company d

chunks.text: Other apps installed on the device must not access Company data or credentials. Apps with overly broad permissions (e.g., file-system-wide access) are flagged by MDM.

sections.section_heading: Public AI tools (e.g., ChatGPT, public Claude, Gemini consum

chunks.text: Public AI tools (e.g., ChatGPT, public Claude, Gemini consumer apps) may be installed for personal use but may not be used to process Company Confidential or Restricted data (see SEC-201).

sections.section_heading: Domestic travel

chunks.text: Domestic travel. Devices should be locked when unattended.

sections.section_heading: International travel

chunks.text: International travel.

Devices crossing borders may be subject to inspection. Employees traveling to high-risk destinations (refer to Travel Risk listings) may be issued loaner travel devices upon request; these are wiped on return.

sections.section_heading: Powering off

chunks.text: Powering off. Devices crossing international borders should be powered off (not just locked) prior to inspection.

sections.section_heading: Lost or stolen devices must be reported to security@northwin

chunks.text: Lost or stolen devices must be reported to security@northwindlogistics.com or by phone immediately.

sections.section_heading: The Company's MDM enables remote wipe of the Company partiti

chunks.text: The Company's MDM enables remote wipe of the Company partition. For Company-issued devices, full-device wipe may be triggered.

sections.section_heading: Personal devices that fail compliance checks (e.g., OS out o

chunks.text: Personal devices that fail compliance checks (e.g., OS out of support, rooted) may have their Company partition automatically wiped without further notice. Personal apps and data are preserved.

sections.section_heading: On departure, the Company's MDM removes the Company partitio

chunks.text: On departure, the Company's MDM removes the Company partition from personal devices.

sections.section_heading: Company-issued devices must be returned by the last working

chunks.text: Company-issued devices must be returned by the last working day.

sections.section_heading: The Company does not reimburse employees for personal mobile

chunks.text: The Company does not reimburse employees for personal mobile device hardware or carrier plans, except where a Company-issued device cannot meet a job requirement.

sections.section_heading: International roaming charges incurred during business trave

chunks.text: International roaming charges incurred during business travel are reimbursable per the Travel & Expense Policy framework.

sections.section_heading: The Company's MDM platform reports device compliance state,

chunks.text: The Company's MDM platform reports device compliance state, OS version, MDM-managed app inventory, and approximate location of Company-issued devices.

sections.section_heading: On personal devices, the MDM platform does NOT report person

chunks.text: On personal devices, the MDM platform does NOT report personal app inventory, photos, messages, browsing history, or contacts.

sections.section_heading: The Privacy Notice for MDM (PRIV-105) describes the data col

chunks.text: The Privacy Notice for MDM (PRIV-105) describes the data collected by the MDM platform in detail.

sections.section_heading: Definitions

chunks.text: • MDM — Mobile Device Management; a platform enabling configuration, monitoring, and remote actions on enrolled devices. • BYOD — Bring Your Own Device. • Company partition — A separated, MDM-controlled set of apps and data on a personal device. • Jailbreak / root — Modification of a device's operating system to remove vendor-imposed restrictions, weakening device security.

chunks.text: Sustainability and Environmental Responsibility Policy Document: SUS-001 Version: 2.1 Effective Date: January 1, 2025 Owner: Office of Sustainability Applies To: All Northwind Logistics employees and operating facilities

sections.section_heading: Purpose

chunks.text: Northwind Logistics is committed to operating in a manner that minimizes environmental impact, supports the communities in which we operate, and contributes to the broader transition to a low- carbon economy. This policy articulates our environmental commitments and the operational expectations they create.

sections.section_heading: Scope

chunks.text: This policy applies to all Company-owned and Company-operated facilities, all Company vehicles and fleet operations, all employee business activities, and all Company procurement decisions where environmental considerations are material.

sections.section_heading: The Company has committed to a 50% reduction in Scope 1 and

chunks.text: The Company has committed to a 50% reduction in Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions by 2030, against a 2019 baseline, and net-zero by 2045. Progress is reported annually in the Sustainability Report.

sections.section_heading: Each facility manager is responsible for tracking and report

chunks.text: Each facility manager is responsible for tracking and reporting monthly energy consumption (electricity, natural gas, fuel) through the SustainTrack platform.

sections.section_heading: New equipment purchases over $5,000 must include an energy-e

chunks.text: New equipment purchases over $5,000 must include an energy-efficiency comparison and, where commercially reasonable, the more efficient option should be selected.

sections.section_heading: All offices will provide recycling collection for paper, car

chunks.text: All offices will provide recycling collection for paper, cardboard, glass, aluminum, and plastics #1 and #2.

sections.section_heading: E-waste must be routed through the Company's approved e-wast

chunks.text: E-waste must be routed through the Company's approved e-waste recycler. Disposal of e-waste in general trash is prohibited.

sections.section_heading: Single-use plastics (water bottles, utensils, plates) should

chunks.text: Single-use plastics (water bottles, utensils, plates) should be avoided in catered meetings and Company events. Bulk-water dispensers and reusable serviceware are preferred.

sections.section_heading: Employees should consider the carbon impact of business trav

chunks.text: Employees should consider the carbon impact of business travel when selecting itineraries. Direct flights, ground transportation, and video meetings should be considered where the business case permits.

sections.section_heading: Personal vehicle use for business is subject to the Ground T

chunks.text: Personal vehicle use for business is subject to the Ground Transportation Policy (TEP-006); this policy does not modify those rules.

sections.section_heading: The Company offers pre-tax commuter benefits for public tran

chunks.text: The Company offers pre-tax commuter benefits for public transit and a workplace EV charging stipend at sites where chargers are installed.

sections.section_heading: Procurement teams should consider environmental factors (ven

chunks.text: Procurement teams should consider environmental factors (vendor sustainability disclosures, packaging waste, transportation distance) alongside cost and quality.

sections.section_heading: Office supply orders should default to recycled-content pape

chunks.text: Office supply orders should default to recycled-content paper and refillable products where available.

sections.section_heading: The Office of Sustainability publishes quarterly internal da

chunks.text: The Office of Sustainability publishes quarterly internal dashboards covering energy consumption, waste diverted from landfill, business-travel emissions, and water usage.

sections.section_heading: Material environmental incidents (spills, regulatory notices

chunks.text: Material environmental incidents (spills, regulatory notices, complaints) must be reported to the Office of Sustainability within 24 hours of discovery.

sections.section_heading: New hires receive sustainability awareness training as part

chunks.text: New hires receive sustainability awareness training as part of onboarding.

sections.section_heading: Facility managers receive annual refresher training on envir

chunks.text: Facility managers receive annual refresher training on environmental compliance, energy reporting, and waste handling.

sections.section_heading: The Office of Sustainability reports to the Chief Operating

chunks.text: The Office of Sustainability reports to the Chief Operating Officer.

sections.section_heading: An employee Sustainability Council, with representation from

chunks.text: An employee Sustainability Council, with representation from each business unit, advises on programs and reviews progress quarterly.
