Reyes, Chief Operating Officer Related documents: TEP-006 Ground Transportation, FAC-003 Facility Operations Manual, PROC-001 Procurement Standards Vendor Onboarding and Third-Party Risk Standard Document: PROC-002 Version: 1.7 Effective Date: January 1, 2025 Owner: Procurement & Vendor Management Applies To: All Northwind Logistics business units engaging third parties

sections.section_heading: Purpose

chunks.text: This standard establishes the process by which new vendors and third parties are onboarded to do business with the Company, the risk reviews required prior to onboarding, and the ongoing oversight expectations for active vendor relationships.

sections.section_heading: Scope

chunks.text: The standard applies to any third party paid or otherwise compensated by the Company in exchange for goods, services, software, data, or rights, including contractors, consultants, software-as-a- service providers, distributors, and resellers. The standard does not apply to: employee expense reimbursements (governed by TEP-001), customer-facing transactions, charitable donations, or government filings.

sections.section_heading: Initiation

chunks.text: A business sponsor submits a Vendor Onboarding Request in the Procurement Portal, including: vendor name, services or goods sought, estimated annual spend, data access required, and proposed term.

sections.section_heading: Risk classification

chunks.text: Vendors are classified as Tier A, B, or C based on annual spend, data access, and operational criticality.

The classification drives the depth of due diligence. Due diligence Tier Criteria depth Spend > $500k/year OR access to Confidential/Restricted data OR A Full review critical operational dependency B Spend $50k–$500k/year OR access to Internal data Standard review C Spend < $50k/year AND no data access AND non-critical Light review

sections.section_heading: Due diligence

chunks.text: Standard and full reviews include vendor questionnaire, financial review (Bradstreet or equivalent), security questionnaire (SIG-Lite or equivalent), insurance certificate review, and watchlist screening (OFAC, EU sanctions, PEPs). Full reviews additionally include on-site or virtual site visit, SOC 2 Type II review (or equivalent), and reference checks with at least two existing customers of similar profile.

sections.section_heading: Contract

chunks.text: All vendor engagements require a written agreement. Standard agreements include the Company's standard terms; deviations require Legal review.

sections.section_heading: Onboarding

chunks.text: Approved vendors are added to the vendor master with a unique vendor ID, banking details, and payment terms (default Net 30, calendar days).

sections.section_heading: Tier A vendors are reviewed annually; Tier B every 18 months

chunks.text: Tier A vendors are reviewed annually; Tier B every 18 months; Tier C every 3 years or on material change.

sections.section_heading: Material changes (ownership, location, business model, secur

chunks.text: Material changes (ownership, location, business model, security posture) trigger an out-of-cycle review.

sections.section_heading: Annual security questionnaire renewal is required for any ve

chunks.text: Annual security questionnaire renewal is required for any vendor with Internal-or-above data access.

sections.section_heading: Payment to vendors is processed by Accounts Payable based on

chunks.text: Payment to vendors is processed by Accounts Payable based on approved invoices matched to purchase orders.

sections.section_heading: Invoices missing a valid PO are routed to the business spons

chunks.text: Invoices missing a valid PO are routed to the business sponsor for confirmation before payment.

sections.section_heading: Wire instructions changes from a vendor require independent

chunks.text: Wire instructions changes from a vendor require independent verbal verification with a known contact at the vendor before any payment is sent to the new account. This is a fraud control and may not be bypassed under time pressure.

sections.section_heading: When a vendor relationship ends, the business sponsor initia

chunks.text: When a vendor relationship ends, the business sponsor initiates an Offboarding Request, including final invoice cutoff, data return or destruction requirements, system access removal, and confirmation that all deliverables have been received.

sections.section_heading: Vendors with Confidential or Restricted data access must pro

chunks.text: Vendors with Confidential or Restricted data access must provide a Certificate of Destruction within 30 days of offboarding.

sections.section_heading: The Approved Vendor List, maintained in the Procurement Port

chunks.text: The Approved Vendor List, maintained in the Procurement Portal, identifies preferred vendors for common categories. Use of an Approved Vendor satisfies the onboarding requirement for Tier B and C engagements.

sections.section_heading: Adding a vendor to the Approved Vendor List requires CFO sig

chunks.text: Adding a vendor to the Approved Vendor List requires CFO sign-off following full due diligence.

sections.section_heading: Exceptions

chunks.text: Exceptions to this standard require written approval from the Chief Procurement Officer. Emergency engagements (operational continuity, security incident response) may proceed under expedited review with retrospective documentation within 10 business days.

sections.section_heading: Definitions

chunks.text: • Vendor — A third party providing goods or services to the Company under a contract or PO. • Purchase order (PO) — A document authorizing a vendor to deliver goods or services and committing the Company to a defined payment. • Bradstreet — Dun & Bradstreet credit and risk reporting service. • OFAC — U.S. Office of Foreign Assets Control sanctions list.

sections.section_heading: Purpose

chunks.text: The Company believes its long-term success is connected to the well-being of the communities in which it operates. This policy describes the programs through which the Company and its employees contribute to charitable causes — financial donations, matching gifts, volunteer time, and pro-bono service.

sections.section_heading: Scope

chunks.text: This policy covers Company-sponsored charitable activity and employee participation programs. It does not cover personal charitable activity unrelated to the Company.

sections.section_heading: The Company matches employee donations to eligible nonprofit

chunks.text: The Company matches employee donations to eligible nonprofit organizations, 1:1, up to $5,000 per employee per calendar year.

sections.section_heading: Eligible organizations are those qualified as 501(c)(3) publ

chunks.text: Eligible organizations are those qualified as 501(c)(3) public charities in the United States, or the local equivalent in other countries where the Company has operations (registered charities in the UK, Canadian Registered Charities, etc.).

sections.section_heading: Ineligible recipients include political organizations, candi

chunks.text: Ineligible recipients include political organizations, candidates, religious organizations except where the funds support a non-religious charitable purpose, and lobbying organizations.

sections.section_heading: Employees submit match requests through the Bright Funds pla

chunks.text: Employees submit match requests through the Bright Funds platform within 60 days of the donation.

sections.section_heading: Full-time employees receive 16 hours of paid Volunteer Time

chunks.text: Full-time employees receive 16 hours of paid Volunteer Time Off per calendar year for participation in approved volunteer activities.

sections.section_heading: Part-time employees receive a prorated VTO allocation based

chunks.text: Part-time employees receive a prorated VTO allocation based on standard hours.

sections.section_heading: VTO is requested through Workday with the volunteer organiza

chunks.text: VTO is requested through Workday with the volunteer organization and activity noted.

sections.section_heading: Employees who volunteer 25+ hours with an eligible organizat

chunks.text: Employees who volunteer 25+ hours with an eligible organization in a calendar year receive a $250 grant for that organization, payable through the matching gift platform.

sections.section_heading: Multiple grants per year are permitted, capped at $1,000 per

chunks.text: Multiple grants per year are permitted, capped at $1,000 per employee per year.

sections.section_heading: During designated disaster relief events, the Company may en

chunks.text: During designated disaster relief events, the Company may enhance the standard match (e.g., 2:1 instead of 1:1) for donations to approved relief organizations.

sections.section_heading: Enhanced match events are announced via internal communicati

chunks.text: Enhanced match events are announced via internal communications and are time-bounded.

sections.section_heading: The Company encourages employees to serve on nonprofit board

chunks.text: The Company encourages employees to serve on nonprofit boards as a form of community engagement.

sections.section_heading: Reasonable time during business hours for board service may

chunks.text: Reasonable time during business hours for board service may be approved by the employee's manager (typically up to 8 hours per quarter).

sections.section_heading: Board service that creates a conflict of interest with the C

chunks.text: Board service that creates a conflict of interest with the Company's business must be disclosed under the Code of Conduct (COC-001 §4).

sections.section_heading: The Company periodically organizes group volunteer activitie

chunks.text: The Company periodically organizes group volunteer activities, fundraising drives, and partnerships with nonprofit organizations. Participation in these activities is voluntary.

sections.section_heading: Time spent participating in Company-organized activities dur

chunks.text: Time spent participating in Company-organized activities during normal working hours is paid and does not count against VTO.

sections.section_heading: Company-led activities are coordinated by the Community Enga

chunks.text: Company-led activities are coordinated by the Community Engagement team and announced through internal channels.

sections.section_heading: Full-time and part-time employees in active status are eligi

chunks.text: Full-time and part-time employees in active status are eligible.

sections.section_heading: Matching gifts and Dollars-for-Doers grants are made directl

chunks.text: Matching gifts and Dollars-for-Doers grants are made directly by the Company to the recipient organization. They are not taxable income to the employee.

sections.section_heading: Personal donations are deductible on the employee's individu

chunks.text: Personal donations are deductible on the employee's individual tax return, subject to applicable rules.

sections.section_heading: Charitable activity using Company funds or in a manner that

chunks.text: Charitable activity using Company funds or in a manner that ties the Company's name to a cause requires Community Engagement team coordination.

sections.section_heading: Solicitation of colleagues for personal causes is discourage

chunks.text: Solicitation of colleagues for personal causes is discouraged but not prohibited; however, sustained or aggressive solicitation should be avoided.

sections.section_heading: Use of Company premises for charitable events requires Facil

chunks.text: Use of Company premises for charitable events requires Facilities approval.

sections.section_heading: Political contributions are not eligible for matching, are n

chunks.text: Political contributions are not eligible for matching, are not VTO-eligible, and are addressed separately in the Political Activity Policy (LEG-201).

sections.section_heading: Matching gifts, VTO, and Dollars-for-Doers participation are

chunks.text: Matching gifts, VTO, and Dollars-for-Doers participation are tracked through the Bright Funds platform and Workday.

sections.section_heading: Aggregate community impact is reported annually in the Compa

chunks.text: Aggregate community impact is reported annually in the Company's Sustainability Report.

sections.section_heading: Definitions

chunks.text: • VTO — Volunteer Time Off. • Eligible organization — A nonprofit organization that meets the criteria in Section 3.1.2. • Bright Funds — The Company's matching gift and giving platform vendor.

sections.section_heading: Purpose

chunks.text: Business continuity ensures the Company can maintain or rapidly restore critical operations during and after a disruption. This summary describes the overall framework; the full Business Continuity Plan (BCP) and unit-specific runbooks are maintained by the Business Continuity Office and shared with relevant personnel on a need-to-know basis.

sections.section_heading: Scope

chunks.text: The framework covers all Company operations and critical third-party dependencies. Disruption types include — but are not limited to — natural disasters, cybersecurity incidents, physical site loss, key- vendor failure, pandemic, and prolonged staffing disruption.

sections.section_heading: Critical functions

chunks.text: The Company's Business Impact Analysis identifies the following functions as critical, with their target Recovery Time Objectives (RTO) and Recovery Point Objectives (RPO): Function RTO RPO Customer order intake 4 hours 15 minutes Active shipment tracking 2 hours 5 minutes Customer support contact (phone, email, chat) 8 hours 1 hour Payroll processing 5 business days 1 business day Accounts payable 10 business days 1 business day Financial reporting (period-close) 5 business days 1 business day Corporate email and collaboration 4 hours 15 minutes Non-critical functions have looser RTO targets and are restored after critical functions are stable.

sections.section_heading: Business Continuity Office (BCO) — Maintains the BCP, coordi

chunks.text: Business Continuity Office (BCO) — Maintains the BCP, coordinates exercises, declares plan activation, reports to executive leadership.

sections.section_heading: Crisis Management Team (CMT) — Activated during major events

chunks.text: Crisis Management Team (CMT) — Activated during major events. Composed of: CEO (or designated successor), COO, CFO, General Counsel, CHRO, CISO, CIO, Head of Operations.

sections.section_heading: Functional Lead — For each critical function, a named functi

chunks.text: Functional Lead — For each critical function, a named functional lead and at least one backup lead.

sections.section_heading: Crisis Communications Lead — Coordinates internal and extern

chunks.text: Crisis Communications Lead — Coordinates internal and external messaging during a plan activation. Default: VP, Communications.

sections.section_heading: The BCP is activated by the BCO Director or, in their absenc

chunks.text: The BCP is activated by the BCO Director or, in their absence, by any member of the Crisis Management Team.

sections.section_heading: Activation triggers: significant disruption affecting one or

chunks.text: Activation triggers: significant disruption affecting one or more critical functions, or anticipated to affect them within 24 hours.

sections.section_heading: Activation is communicated through the Emergency Notificatio

chunks.text: Activation is communicated through the Emergency Notification System and via direct outreach to the CMT.

sections.section_heading: Internal communication

chunks.text: Internal communication.

The Emergency Notification System sends initial alerts. Subsequent updates use a combination of email, internal messaging, and (if available) a dedicated incident page on intranet.northwindlogistics.com/incident.

sections.section_heading: External communication

chunks.text: External communication.
Customer communications go through Customer Operations and Communications jointly. Media communications go through Communications and Legal. Regulator communications go through Legal.

sections.section_heading: Status cadence

chunks.text: Status cadence. During plan activation, the CMT meets at least daily; functional updates are due daily by 4 PM local time.

sections.section_heading: The Company's workforce can largely operate remotely

chunks.text: The Company's workforce can largely operate remotely. Roles requiring on-site presence (warehouse, distribution center, certain field operations) have backup-site arrangements.

sections.section_heading: During pandemic-style disruptions, remote-first protocols ca

chunks.text: During pandemic-style disruptions, remote-first protocols can be activated company-wide.

sections.section_heading: Critical roles have designated successors trained on essenti

chunks.text: Critical roles have designated successors trained on essential procedures.

sections.section_heading: Critical systems are deployed in cloud configurations suppor

chunks.text: Critical systems are deployed in cloud configurations supporting cross-region failover with documented procedures.

sections.section_heading: Backup and recovery testing is conducted quarterly for criti

chunks.text: Backup and recovery testing is conducted quarterly for critical systems and annually for non- critical.

sections.section_heading: Cybersecurity incident response is coordinated by Security p

chunks.text: Cybersecurity incident response is coordinated by Security per the Incident Response Plan (SEC- IR-001) and integrated with the BCP for incidents materially affecting operations.

sections.section_heading: Tier A vendors (per PROC-002) have business continuity asses

chunks.text: Tier A vendors (per PROC-002) have business continuity assessment as part of due diligence.

sections.section_heading: Single-source dependencies are tracked by the BCO; mitigatio

chunks.text: Single-source dependencies are tracked by the BCO; mitigation includes contractual SLAs, backup arrangements, and inventory buffering as appropriate.

sections.section_heading: The Company maintains an alternate corporate site arrangemen

chunks.text: The Company maintains an alternate corporate site arrangement (currently Dallas, TX as alternate to the Irvine HQ).

sections.section_heading: Distribution centers have mutual-aid arrangements with siste

chunks.text: Distribution centers have mutual-aid arrangements with sister facilities for re-routing shipments.

sections.section_heading: The BCP undergoes a tabletop exercise at least annually with

chunks.text: The BCP undergoes a tabletop exercise at least annually with the CMT.

sections.section_heading: Full-scale exercises (simulated incident, real workforce res

chunks.text: Full-scale exercises (simulated incident, real workforce response) are conducted biennially.

sections.section_heading: After-action reviews follow every plan activation and every

chunks.text: After-action reviews follow every plan activation and every significant exercise.

sections.section_heading: The BCP is reviewed annually and updated whenever a material

chunks.text: The BCP is reviewed annually and updated whenever a material change in operations, technology, or risk profile occurs.

sections.section_heading: Definitions

chunks.text: • RTO — Recovery Time Objective; the targeted duration of time within which a business function must be restored after disruption. • RPO — Recovery Point Objective; the maximum acceptable amount of data loss (measured in time). • BIA — Business Impact Analysis; an assessment of the operational and financial impact of disruption to each function. • CMT — Crisis Management Team.
